reasoning:
The FRED series RPONTSYD is not published on weekends. For the base date 2025-10-26 (Sunday), standard resolution rules use the most recent prior value: Friday, 2025-10-24, which the FRED data shows is exactly 0.0. Therefore, any positive value (>0) on the target dates represents an increase. 
1) For 2025-11-02 (Sunday), the relevant date is Friday, 2025-10-31. While Fridays often see $0.0$ usage due to weekend interest costs, Oct 31 is a month-end day nestled in a documented October 2025 funding crunch. Dealers frequently scramble for liquidity for window-dressing on month-ends, making a positive usage highly likely (predicted ~64%). 
2) For 2025-11-25 (Tuesday), Tuesdays historically have a much higher base rate of non-zero SRF usage (either direct borrowing or $1M test trades). The predicted probability stays moderately high at 57%. 
3) For 2026-01-24 (Saturday), the reference is Friday, 2026-01-23. Funding strains may have abated by then, and ordinary Fridays overwhelmingly exhibit 0 usage. However, reflecting the instruction to push distant forecasts closer to 0.5 to model increasing uncertainty, I estimate 48%.
